title: The Era of Token-Based Competition Is Here. Is Your AI Strategy Ready?
gist: BCG introduces 'token-based competition' as the successor to time-based competition (1988): among 107 public tech companies, those in the highest token-use quintile grew revenue 16.5% YoY vs. 5.1% for the lowest quintile, suggesting AI token deployment is already translating into measurable competitive advantage—with ROInt (return on intelligence) proposed as the new tracking metric.

themes.theme: Token Consumption as a Competitive Signal

insights.claim: BCG's analysis of 107 public technology companies with >$500M trailing revenue found that firms in the highest AI token-use quintile had 16.5% median year-over-year revenue growth versus 5.1% in the lowest-usage group—the first empirical signal that productive token use translates into measurable competitive advantage.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Token consumption is emerging as a leading indicator of revenue growth for tech companies; investors should begin asking management teams for disclosure of AI token deployment rates and ROInt metrics.
insights.quote: companies in the highest token-use quintile had 16.5% median year-over-year revenue growth, compared with 5.1% in the lowest-usage group
insights.locator: We analyzed token consumption at 107 public technology companies

insights.claim: BCG proposes 'token-based competition' as a new basis for competitive advantage—analogous to the 'time-based competition' concept BCG introduced in 1988—where intelligence is now abundant through AI, and advantage comes from applying it to business problems more productively than competitors.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: The strategic paradigm is shifting from 'who has the best talent' to 'who deploys AI most productively'; companies that design workflows around AI-human collaboration rather than pure headcount will compound advantages over time.
insights.quote: We have entered a new era of competition. When intelligence was the sole domain of humans, firms with a monopoly on scarce talent could sustain a clear advantage.
insights.locator: We have entered a new era of competition.

insights.claim: AI-enabled processes create a flywheel: they get smarter, faster, and cheaper as underlying models improve and systems learn from every interaction; companies that build the right AI-powered workflows now will see those workflows improve organically over time even without additional investment.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: First-mover advantage in AI workflow design compounds over time—each deployment improves model quality and system learning, widening the gap between early adopters and laggards.
insights.locator: AI-enabled processes get smarter, faster, and cheaper

insights.claim: BCG proposes ROInt (return on intelligence) as the primary metric—value of output divided by combined cost of labor and tokens—which lets companies compare very different AI applications on the same basis; measuring only labor savings systematically favors narrower uses, while 'tokenmaxxing' creates perverse incentives.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: CFOs who track AI ROI purely through headcount reduction will misallocate AI investment; adopting ROInt enables apples-to-apples comparison between automation plays (cost reduction) and augmentation plays (revenue and quality improvement).
insights.quote: ROInt lets companies compare very different AI applications on the same basis: what value is the company getting from the combined use of people and tokens?

insights.claim: Reckitt reported up to 60% faster content development, faster research cycles with fewer prototypes, and higher-quality marketing and innovation outputs after applying AI selectively to marketing, product development, and R&D—illustrating high-ROInt deployment in a consumer goods context.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Consumer goods companies have a proven AI deployment playbook in marketing/R&D with measurable ROI; Reckitt's approach (AI in high-value processes to accelerate insight-to-revenue) is a template applicable across the sector.
insights.quote: Reckitt has reported up to 60% faster content development, faster research cycles with fewer prototypes, and higher-quality marketing and innovation outputs
insights.locator: a UK-based consumer goods company, Reckitt

insights.claim: Gartner predicts half of companies that cut customer service staff due to AI will rehire by 2027; in a survey of 321 customer service leaders, only 20% have legitimately reduced staffing—suggesting AI substitution in customer service is overstated and that human context, relationships, and physical presence remain critical.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Companies aggressively cutting customer-facing headcount based on AI hype risk costly rehiring cycles; the playbook of augmenting (rather than replacing) customer service staff is more defensible.
insights.quote: Gartner predicts that half of companies that cut customer service staff due to AI will rehire by 2027
insights.locator: Treat tokens as talent enhancers, not just as labor substitutes.

insights.claim: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince argued AI reduces need for 'measuring' roles (middle management, operations) while increasing the value of employees focused on building products and engaging customers—a segmentation of the workforce into creators/customer-facers vs. coordinators.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: The emerging consensus is that AI substitutes for coordination and measurement work while amplifying output of builders and relationship managers—a structural shift in which layers of the org chart face pressure.
insights.locator: Cloudflare, CEO Matthew Prince recently shared the organisation's approach

insights.claim: BCG finds fewer than 10% of employees use AI in an agentic way—delegating multi-step work to AI agents—versus merely prompting for discrete answers; adoption stalls for human reasons: not knowing where AI creates value, falling back on familiar routines, or fearing AI threatens core expertise.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: The gap between AI capability and agentic adoption represents the largest near-term opportunity for enterprise AI value capture; change management and workflow redesign are the binding constraints, not model capability.
insights.quote: fewer than 10% of employees use AI in an agentic way
insights.locator: We know that fewer than 10% of employees use AI in an agentic way
